sections.title: NL-Clicker Plugin API SDK
sections.text: Reference documentation for developing plugins for the NL-Clicker application.

sections.title: DEVELOPMENT SDK
sections.text: The NL-Clicker SDK provides essential stub files to enable IDE support and prevent linting errors during development.

blocks.title: SDK Components
blocks.content: These files act as placeholders (stubs) for the IDE, as the real API is injected by the Core at runtime. Using them ensures your code remains clean and free of false 'ImportError' warnings.

rows: Stubs for PluginContext. Provides IntelliSense for UI, Window, and Data methods.
Stubs for uireg and rdr. Prevents 'ModuleNotFound' errors in local IDE.
Boilerplate reflecting the required plugin structure.
A minimal script for basic syntax verification.
Template metadata file with all required fields.

blocks.title: Manifest Example (manifest.json)
blocks.content: The manifest must be placed in the root of your plugin directory. It defines how the core identifies and loads your module.

blocks.title: JSON Structure
blocks.content:
{
    "id": "unique.plugin.id",
    "name": "Plugin Display Name",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "author": "Developer Name",
    "description": "Brief description of features",
    "main_class": "MyPlugin",
    "min_app_version": "1.0.0",
    "dependencies": ["customtkinter"]
}

blocks.title: Modern Entry Point Example (main.py)
blocks.content: Use 'TYPE_CHECKING' to leverage SDK stubs during development while allowing the core to inject real objects at runtime.

blocks.title: Implementation Code

sections.title: REGISTRY ARCHITECTURE
sections.text: The system utilizes two global repositories (Singletons) to facilitate communication between the plugin and the core.

blocks.title: 1. UIRegistry (Anchor Registry)
blocks.content:
Purpose: Stores references to the physical frames (ctk.CTkFrame) of the main application.
Access: Publicly accessible within the application scope. Plugins access these via string aliases defined by the core or other plugins.

rows: Methods: has, get_anchor, try_get_anchor. These methods provide open access to retrieve stored instances without additional security constraints.
Methods: add_frame, add_frames_list, register_auto_incremental, register_by_type, register_frames_list, remove_frame. Used for manual or automated frame registration.
Registers a single CTkFrame under a unique key. Validates if the name is already taken.
Batch registers a dictionary {name: frame}. Performs pre-validation on all elements.
Generates a unique key (e.g., frame_1) and registers the frame automatically.
Uses the class name as a key, adding suffixes if the name exists.
Takes a list of frames and registers them using auto-incremental naming.
Removes a frame by name and returns it.
Checks if a name is registered.
Returns a frame by name or None.
Safely returns a frame or a fallback value.

blocks.title: 2. RedactableDataRegistry (Data Registry)
blocks.content: Purpose: A universal store for class objects, modules, or primitive variables. Features strict caller validation.

rows: Methods: get, try_get. Strictly restricted to 'PluginContext' callers. Direct access from Global Scope is forbidden.
Methods: bind, bind_batch, bind_auto, bind_by_type. Open for data sharing between system components.
Registers a value with a unique name. Throws KeyError on conflict.
Registers multiple items from a dictionary.
Auto-generates a unique name with a counter.
Registers using the class name as a key.
Retrieves data. Performs 'inspect.stack' validation to verify the caller.

sections.title: PLUGIN LIFECYCLE
sections.text: Understanding how the core triggers plugin logic.

blocks.title: Event Hooks
blocks.content: The PluginManager interacts with your main class through specific method hooks.

rows: The plugin is first recognized and instantiated. Use this only for internal variable setup.
Called immediately after loading or when the user clicks 'Run' in the Manager. This is the place to mount UI and start logic.

sections.title: PLUGIN CONTEXT API
sections.text: The PluginContext class serves as the primary interface for plugins to interact with the core application. It acts as a secure sandbox and service provider, granting access to UI mounting, window management, and persistent storage.

blocks.title: Core Concept
blocks.content: Each plugin receives its own unique instance of PluginContext upon initialization. This instance is pre-configured with the plugin's ID, dedicated data paths, and a reference to the main application's resource importer.

rows: The unique identifier of the plugin.
Path to the plugin's dedicated folder for local storage and configs.
Path to the plugin's .zip source for internal resource loading.
A property returning the reference to the main application instance.

blocks.title: Initialization & Environment
blocks.content: When a plugin is loaded, the context automatically creates its data directory and initializes a zipimporter for resource management. This ensures that the plugin is 'ready-to-go' without manual environment setup.

sections.title: UI INTERFACE
sections.text: Methods for mounting, managing, and disposing of user interface elements within the core application or dedicated plugin windows.

blocks.title: Mounting & Layout
blocks.content: The system uses a 'SmartLayout' manager to handle widget positioning and Z-index (weight). This allows multiple plugins to share the same anchor point without overlapping issues.

rows:
Mounts a widget into a core frame.
- anchor_name (str): Target alias from UIRegistry.
- widget_cls: The class to instantiate.
- weight (int): Lower values appear first. -1 appends to the end.
- **kwargs: Standard CTk arguments.
Instantiates and adds a widget to the current plugin's Toplevel window using the internal SmartLayout.
Clears the plugin window and mounts a single container (like a ScrollableFrame) as the primary content area.

blocks.title: SmartLayout Engine
blocks.content: All UI mounting is handled by SmartLayout, which ensures widgets are correctly ordered even when added by different plugins.

rows: Low priority. Appends the widget to the bottom of the list (Default).
High priority. Stays at the very top (e.g., headers).
Sorted by the order they were added (Internal counter).

blocks.title: Cleanup & Destruction
blocks.content: Proper unmounting is crucial for preventing memory leaks and ghost widgets when a plugin is disabled or reloaded.

rows: Destroys a specific widget and removes its reference from the internal tracking list and layout manager.
Iterates through all spawned widgets in reverse order and destroys them. Automatically called during plugin shutdown.
Destroys all children within the dedicated plugin window and resets its internal layout registry.

sections.title: PLUGIN WINDOW MANAGEMENT
sections.text: Comprehensive controls for the plugin's dedicated Toplevel window.

blocks.title: Lifecycle & State
blocks.content: The plugin window is lazily initialized via 'get_window'. It remains transient to the main application, meaning it stays above it or closes with it.

rows: Creates or retrieves the window. If 'fix_size' (e.g., '600x400') is provided, resizing is disabled.
Overrides the WM_DELETE_WINDOW protocol. Useful for custom cleanup or confirmation dialogs.
Uses deiconify/withdraw to toggle visibility without destroying the window state.
Triggers 'unmount_all' and destroys the window instance. Resets the internal reference to None.

blocks.title: Geometry & Appearance
blocks.content: Methods for precise positioning and visual behavior of the window.

rows: Calculates and sets the window position exactly at the center of the Main Application's current geometry.
Toggles the '-topmost' attribute. If True, the window stays above all other application windows.
Returns a tuple (x, y, width, height) of the current window state.
Moves the window to specific screen coordinates using geometry offsets.
Updates the window title bar text dynamically.

sections.title: DATA & RESOURCES
sections.text: Methods for interacting with the plugin's package resources, persistent storage, and remote registry synchronization.

blocks.title: Remote Data Synchronization
blocks.content: The 'update_remote_value' method is a powerful tool for reactive data binding between the plugin and core objects stored in the RedactableDataRegistry.

rows: Updates a target attribute. Supports both direct assignment and .set() methods (for CTk/Tk variables). Validates that the 'new_value' type matches the current one to prevent runtime crashes.

blocks.title: Package & File System
blocks.content: Plugins operate within a dedicated environment, using a zip-importer for internal assets and a specific AppData subdirectory for user configs.

rows: Extracts file content as bytes directly from the plugin's .nlp (ZIP) archive without manual extraction.
Serializes a dictionary to JSON and saves it to the plugin's 'data_path' (local AppData). Ensures the directory exists before saving.
Reads and parses a JSON config file. Returns an empty dictionary if the file is missing or corrupted.
Properties providing direct Path objects to the plugin's working directory and source package.

sections.title: SERVICE & UTILITIES
sections.text: Auxiliary tools for system logging and direct core access.

blocks.title: Logging & Core Access
blocks.content: The PluginContext provides shortcuts to essential system services like the centralized logger and the main application instance.

rows: Submits a formatted message to the core logging engine. Supported levels: INFO (general), WARN (non-critical issues), ERROR (failures).
Returns a direct reference to the core CTk application. Essential when creating custom Toplevel windows or performing global UI operations.

sections.title: QUICK START GUIDE
sections.text: Follow these steps to build, package, and deploy your first functional plugin for the system.

blocks.title: 1. Directory Structure
blocks.content:
MyPlugin/
├── manifest.json      # Metadata and entry point definition
├── main.py            # Main logic and UI mounting
├── assets/            # Internal resources (images, icons, etc.)
└── libs/              # External python modules or binary extensions

blocks.title: 2. Manifest (manifest.json)
3. Entry Point (main.py)
4. Deployment
blocks.content:
1. Select all files inside your project folder.
2. Compress them into a ZIP archive.
3. Change the file extension from .zip to .nlp.
4. Move the file to: %APPDATA%/NL-clicker/plugins/
5. Restart the application to load the plugin.

sections.title: EXTERNAL LIBRARIES
sections.text: The system features an automated dependency manager for external Python modules.

blocks.title: The 'libs' folder
blocks.content:
If your plugin requires third-party libraries (e.g., requests, PIL, or custom .pyd binaries), place them in a /libs folder inside your .nlp archive.

Upon loading, the PluginManager will:
1. Extract these libraries to the plugin's dedicated data folder.
2. Automatically inject the path into 'sys.path'.
3. Re-extract only if the plugin version in manifest.json changes.
